div.h1: Alert Threshold Tuning: Sensitivity vs Noise

p.strong: Finding the balance between early detection and alert fatigue

g.text: p=0.001 | FPR: 0.05 | Alerts/day: 6 | Risk: May miss real drift
p=0.05 | FPR: 2.50 | Alerts/day: 300 | Risk: Alert fatigue
p=0.10 | FPR: 5.00 | Alerts/day: 600 | Risk: Engineers ignore
Expected false alerts = features × FPR × checks_per_day
< 20 alerts/day | HIGH attention | All alerts investigated
20-100 alerts/day | MEDIUM attention | Some alerts skipped
100-500 alerts/day | LOW attention | Most alerts ignored
> 500 alerts/day | ZERO attention | System effectively disabled

div.p: Expected False Alerts per Day: E[alerts] = N_features × α × N_checks Where:

ul.li: N_checks = drift checks per day (e.g., 48 for 30-min intervals) Example (p=0.01, 50 features, 48 checks/day): E = 50 × 0.01 × 48 = 24 false alerts/day This is at the edge of manageable alert volume.

div.p: Bonferroni Correction (Optional): If monitoring many features, consider: α_adjusted = α / N_features For 50 features at α=0.05: α_adjusted = 0.05 / 50 = 0.001 This is very conservative—may miss real drift. Often better to use aggregate rules instead.
